atoms.semantic_claim: The payload crate adds a dependency on the umbrella `ritual-async` crate.
atoms.notes: No downstream source usage of `ritual-async` was found under `crates/ethereum/payload/src`; the v2.2 port instead depends directly on concrete `ritual-async-*` crates.

atoms.semantic_claim: The payload crate depends on `revm-context` for async execution context support.

destinations.evidence: The same crate is still wired into the payload builder, but the port pins it by path and enables the async/no-base-fee/fee-charge feature set directly.
Destination runtime code still imports and uses `revm_context` APIs.

atoms.equivalence_argument: The dependency survived the port with stronger explicit feature wiring rather than a plain workspace reference, so the async-context capability is preserved through a different manifest shape.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Build and execute a payload path that creates an async context for a scheduled async transaction; failure to compile or missing async metadata propagation would falsify the mapping.
atoms.human_question: Confirm that converting the plain workspace `revm-context` dependency into an explicit path-plus-features edge is sufficient to preserve the downstream async payload context behavior.
atoms.notes: Non-exact because manifest shape changed from workspace inheritance to explicit path-plus-features.

atoms.semantic_claim: The payload crate gains ABI encoding/decoding support via `alloy-sol-types`.

destinations.evidence: The same dependency exists in the destination manifest.
Destination async payload logic actively uses `alloy-sol-types` for ABI work.

atoms.negative_test_candidate: Feed malformed async precompile calldata into `validate_async_commitment_call`; if ABI decoding no longer behaves as expected, the mapping is false.
atoms.notes: Exact manifest carryover with live destination usage.

atoms.negative_test_candidate: Run a payload-building scenario containing scheduled transactions plus async precompile calls and verify the builder still performs async inspection, fee handling, and scheduled verification instead of silently downgrading to plain txpool behavior.
atoms.notes: This excludes the other Ritual additions that were not carried over verbatim.

atoms.semantic_claim: The payload crate adds Ritual metrics/tracing instrumentation support.
atoms.required_change: Restore or intentionally reject payload-local `ritual-metrics` instrumentation for the downstream Ritual payload paths that currently have no counterpart in the port crate.
atoms.notes: Downstream source uses `ritual_metrics::*` heavily, but no corresponding dependency or payload-local instrumentation was found in the v2.2 destination crate.

atoms.semantic_claim: The payload crate depends on sequencing-rights logic to classify and reorder candidate transactions before execution.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Construct a mixed block candidate where fee order conflicts with sequencing-rights order; if the destination builder cannot reproduce the downstream reordered execution order, this atom is unported.
atoms.required_change: Restore or intentionally replace sequencing-rights-based transaction classification and reordering in the payload build path.
atoms.notes: Downstream `src/execution.rs` imports `classify_top_level_with_sr`, `reorder_block`, and `SequencingRightsReader`; no equivalent destination code was found in the port or dependency repos searched.

atoms.semantic_claim: The payload crate keeps heartbeat revival/tracking state in-process while building payloads.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Exercise a restart/cold-start scenario with expired agents requiring heartbeat revival; if the destination builder lacks the downstream hydration/skip-state behavior, the mapping fails.
atoms.required_change: Restore or intentionally replace payload-local heartbeat tracking and hydration behavior used during scheduled or async execution.
atoms.notes: Downstream source calls `ritual_heartbeat_tracker::*` for duration memory and tracker hydration, but no equivalent destination dependency or payload-side tracker code was found.

atoms.semantic_claim: The payload crate depends on Ritual scheduling helpers for heartbeat and scheduled transaction orchestration.

destinations.snippet:
//! This crate keeps the useful downstream orchestration without depending on the old `ritual-scheduling` service crate.
use ritual_scheduled_verification::{compute_obligations, fail_closed_predicate_evaluator, filter_obligations_by_predicates, filter_scheduled_for_block_building_full, get_heartbeat_address, get_scheduler_address, ...};
destinations.evidence: The destination extracted scheduling-specific payload helpers into `ritual-scheduled-block-building`, explicitly replacing the old `ritual-scheduling` service-crate dependency.
The payload crate now imports scheduling behavior from the new dependency crate instead of the old `ritual-scheduling` crate.

atoms.equivalence_argument: The destination did not keep the old crate name, but it moved the payload-relevant scheduling logic into a dedicated `ritual-scheduled-block-building` crate and rewired the payload builder to that API.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Create scheduled calls with active async jobs, heartbeat-routed calls, and predicate-gated obligations; the destination should still synthesize or skip scheduled execution transactions according to the moved helper crate.
atoms.human_question: Confirm that `ritual-scheduled-block-building` fully replaces the old payload-local `ritual-scheduling` dependency surface, including heartbeat-routed and predicate-gated orchestration.
atoms.notes: This is a non-exact move; the crate boundary changed even though substantial scheduling behavior remains.
No `once_cell` usage was found in downstream payload source for this crate; this manifest addition appears non-behavioral from the available context.

atoms.semantic_claim: The payload crate enables parallel transaction classification/preparation work using Rayon.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Use a block candidate with many scheduled and async transactions and compare deterministic inclusion/order against downstream; absence of the parallel preparation path or order-preserving logic would falsify the mapping.
atoms.required_change: Restore or intentionally reject the downstream Rayon-backed payload preparation path used for scheduled and async transaction handling.
atoms.notes: Downstream payload code uses `rayon::iter::{IntoParallelRefIterator, ParallelIterator}` in `execution.rs`, `unified_builder.rs`, `unified_execution.rs`, and `lib.rs`; no destination Rayon usage was found in the port payload crate.

atoms.semantic_claim: The payload crate exposes an `async-context` feature gate that forwards async-context support to EVM dependencies.

destinations.snippet:
revm-context = { path = "../../../../ritual-revm-internal/crates/context", features = ["async-context", "optional_no_base_fee", "optional_fee_charge"] }
alloy-evm = { workspace = true, features = ["async-context"] }
destinations.evidence: The destination enables async-context support directly on its dependency edges instead of exposing a crate-local feature stanza.
Destination payload logic still relies on async-context APIs at runtime.

atoms.equivalence_argument: The port removed the top-level feature switch but hard-enabled the same downstream capability through explicit dependency features, so async context support is present without being optional at this crate boundary.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Build the destination crate with default features only and verify async settlement paths still compile and execute; if async context is no longer available without manual feature toggles, the equivalence claim is wrong.
atoms.human_question: Confirm that making async-context always-on in the payload crate is an acceptable replacement for the old opt-in `async-context` feature surface.
atoms.notes: Non-exact because configurability changed from opt-in crate feature to always-on dependency wiring.

atoms.semantic_claim: The payload crate adds a dev-dependency on `alloy-dyn-abi` for local tests or fixtures.
atoms.notes: No `alloy_dyn_abi` usage was found in downstream payload source or tests for this crate.

atoms.semantic_claim: The payload crate adds `secp256k1` as a dev-dependency to support local SPC signature verification tests.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Port the downstream `verify_spc` wrong-output/wrong-signature tests to the destination crate; failure to express those tests without adding `secp256k1` would confirm the gap.
atoms.required_change: Restore or intentionally replace the payload-crate SPC signature test support that depended on `secp256k1`, including wrong-output and wrong-signature regression coverage.
atoms.notes: Downstream `src/lib.rs` contains `tests_verify_spc` using `secp256k1::{Message, Secp256k1, SecretKey}`; the destination moved runtime SPC verification into `src/ritual_async.rs` but no equivalent secp-based tests or dev-dependency were found.

summary: This manifest diff partly ports cleanly into v2.2 for async/scheduled runtime crates, ABI support, and async context wiring, but sequencing-rights, heartbeat tracker behavior, Rayon-backed paths, metrics instrumentation, and SPC test support are not fully accounted for.
notes: Analysis is limited to this manifest diff, but manifest-only atoms were cross-checked against downstream source usage so absent dependencies that back real payload logic are flagged as missing or moved rather than assumed harmless.
